behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git rename detection plus CLI parsing. MOCK-MISS: this is not just one parser function; it depends on how `git` reports rename vs add/delete. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: if tests stub diff output, they can force rename records and miss real failures (for example wrong flags/thresholds causing Git to emit add+delete). Integration is therefore too low. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text has no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit baseline file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo, and assert stdout report includes exactly one rename entry and zero corresponding add/delete entries for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is interaction with real binary content and Git diff behavior. REAL-INFRA: binary handling often fails at subprocess decoding/encoding boundaries and real repo object handling. MOCK-HIDE: mocked text diffs can pass while real binary diffs trigger decode exceptions or parser crashes. Integration is too low unless no subprocess/real git path is involved, which behavior does not state. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified; no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp Git repo, commit a binary file (and optionally modify it), invoke the CLI process, assert it exits successfully and report counts include the binary file in the correct category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real Git behavior for unborn HEAD/no commits and how the CLI handles command exit/status. REAL-INFRA: this depends on actual repository state and Git command semantics. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing a clean response can hide real fatal errors returned by Git in empty repos. Integration is plausible but too low when subprocess behavior is the core risk. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no AI/ML step).
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real Git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert successful exit plus an empty structured report on stdout (no exception/error output as primary result).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history; failure can arise from Git history traversal, diff command choice, and category aggregation across modules. REAL-INFRA: requires real repo state and likely real git process behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked history/diff fixtures can align with implementation assumptions and miss mismatches seen on real history graphs. Integration brake: (a) yes, real subprocess/OS repo state is involved, so upgrade to System. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI/ML output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across multiple commits, run the CLI process, and compare reported category totals to independently computed expected counts from ground-truth git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is Git index vs working-tree semantics, which are real VCS state interactions rather than isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on actual staged/unstaged states in a real repo. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can return equivalent fixtures and hide bugs in how the CLI queries staged vs unstaged diffs. Integration is too low when the key risk is incorrect handling of real git state transitions. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow disqualified; no LLM or multi-agent pipeline behavior in description.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess for both scenarios, and assert the structured stdout reports are byte-for-byte identical (or structurally identical if stable formatting rules apply).
